principles.label: Gender as a Performative Accomplishment
principles.summary: Gender is not an innate, stable identity or an expression of an internal essence, but rather a 'performative accomplishment' constituted through the stylized repetition of bodily acts over time.
principles.details: The expectation of an internal gender essence actually produces the very phenomenon it anticipates. Because gender is not a natural fact, the various acts of gender create the compelling illusion of an abiding gendered self.
principles.representative_quote: gender is in no way a stable identity or locus of agency from which various acts proceede; rather, it is an identity tenuously constituted in time-an identity instituted through a stylized repetition of acts.

principles.label: The Anti-Gender Movement as a Restoration Project
principles.summary: The global attack on 'gender ideology' is not a temporary moral panic, but a reactionary counter-movement designed to destroy progressive legislative gains and restore patriarchal, white supremacist, and colonial power structures.
principles.details: Right-wing movements weaponize 'gender' to redirect anxieties caused by hypercapitalism, deregulated markets, and systemic inequality. By blaming unfamiliar concepts, they falsely promise that eliminating them will restore order and stability.
principles.representative_quote: the anti gender ideology movement is what I would call a counter social movement... its aim is to destroy a movement that it sees as gaining power gaining legislative victories

principles.label: Gender as a Punitive Survival Strategy
principles.summary: Society demands discrete genders to 'humanize' individuals, enforcing compliance through punitive social consequences and the threat of marginalization.
principles.details: The prevailing law uses the threat of getting into 'trouble' to keep people out of trouble, acting as a subtle ruse of power. Those who fail to perform their gender correctly face severe social sanctions.
principles.representative_quote: Hence, as a strategy of survival, gender is a performance with clearly punitive consequences. Discrete genders are part of what 'humanizes' individuals within contemporary culture; indeed, those who fail to do their gender right are regularly punished.

principles.label: The Assault on Gender is an Assault on Democracy
principles.summary: Restricting bodily autonomy and self-definition threatens the core democratic principles of equality, freedom, and justice.
principles.details: Gender freedom is an integral part of any democratic struggle. The pursuit of gender justice is intrinsically linked to the collective struggle against rising authoritarianism.
principles.representative_quote: the assault on gender is also an assault on democracy.

principles.label: The Intertwining of Biology and Environment
principles.summary: The material body and the social environment are deeply intertwined; biology is interactive and constantly activated by environmental and cultural factors.
principles.details: Gender theory does not deny nature, sex, or the materiality of the body. The dualism between culture and biology is outdated, as the environment is constantly taken inside the body through ingestion, breathing, and absorption.
principles.representative_quote: An environmental perspective on these issues would show that many aspects of the human body are deeply affected by the environment. The environment is not just outside: it is always taken inside

principles.label: Ethical Obligation Through Interdependence
principles.summary: Ethical obligations must not rely solely on identification or likeness, but on the recognition of our shared material interdependence and vulnerability.
principles.details: Because we physically share the world, we are inherently intertwined. We must maintain obligations to those who are different, those we fear, or those who have injured us because the world is given to us in common.
principles.representative_quote: This is why I do not think that global obligations can rest on identification... they have to claim us quite regardless of whether or not we feel love or sympathy, for the simple reason that the world is given to us in common

principles.label: The Gender Binary as a Colonial Imposition
principles.summary: The strict two-gender model and the mandate of heterosexual reproductive marriage were imposed by colonial powers and Christian missionaries.
principles.details: The genealogy of gender is inextricably linked to colonial and racist histories. Enforcing a narrow, essentialist perspective of gender helps oppressive systems hold marginalized groups in consistently subordinate positions.
principles.representative_quote: We need to see how Christian missionary views of sex, gender and family have been imposed on Africa. We need to learn a whole lot more about African ways of naming and knowing sex and sexuality that do not conform to the very idea of gender.

principles.label: Agency Within the Scene of Constraint
principles.summary: Agency exists in navigating, improvising, and creatively repeating societal constraints rather than merely adhering to them or possessing absolute freedom.
principles.details: Improvisation provides a 'relative domain of freedom' within rule-bound identity constructions. The unpredictability in how social laws are repeated allows for innovative expressions of identity.

principles.label: Mourning as a Political Act
principles.summary: Mourning mass death and grieving strangers are political acts that forge collectivity and affirm the value of marginalized lives.
principles.details: Grieving a stranger affirms the value of their life and the loss of their potential, creating a shared connection through grief that transcends personal boundaries and highlights systemic inequalities.
principles.representative_quote: Learning to mourn mass death means marking the loss of someone whose name you do not know... the loss that the stranger endures traverses the personal loss one feels, potentially connecting strangers in grief.

principles.label: Joyful Vision Over Righteous Infighting
principles.summary: To counter authoritarianism, progressive movements must articulate a tangible, passionate, and joyful vision of freedom rather than getting bogged down in righteous infighting.
principles.details: Instead of merely critiquing, the left needs to make freedom exciting and sexy, imagining a future of justice and equality that people actually want to build.
principles.representative_quote: Let us imagine freedom in a way we have not before. Make it exciting. Make it sexy.

principles.label: Anti-Trans Feminism as Betrayal
principles.summary: Feminism that excludes trans people betrays the multidimensional commitments and vital solidarities essential to true feminist practice.
principles.details: Restricting the meaning of gender sets up exclusionary norms within feminism, often leading to homophobic consequences and producing new forms of hierarchy.
principles.representative_quote: Anti-trans feminism betrays the multidimensional commitments of feminist theory and practice, including its vital solidarities.

frameworks.label: The Performative Constitution of the Subject
frameworks.summary: A theoretical framework for understanding how identity is formed not from an internal essence, but through the continuous, stylized repetition of social norms and acts.
frameworks.details: This framework posits that power is not merely a top-down force but a pervasive social dynamic that materializes subjects. Agency within this framework is relational, emerging dynamically through interactions and improvisations within a 'scene of constraint.'
frameworks.representative_quote: gender is in no way a stable identity or locus of agency from which various acts proceede; rather, it is an identity tenuously constituted in time-an identity instituted through a stylized repetition of acts.

frameworks.label: Critique of the Psychosocial Phantasm
frameworks.summary: A framework for analyzing how reactionary movements weaponize empty concepts (like 'gender ideology') to absorb and displace societal anxieties caused by systemic economic failures.
frameworks.details: It involves tracking how 'gender' is turned into a demonic or totalitarian threat to mask the devastating effects of neoliberal economic policies, strategic state abandonment, and deregulated capitalism, ultimately serving a 'fantasy of restoration' for patriarchal and white supremacist power.
frameworks.representative_quote: The contradictory character of the phantasm allows it to contain whatever anxiety or fear that the anti-gender ideology wishes to stoke for its own purposes, without having to make any of it cohere.

frameworks.label: Ethics of Cohabitation and Interdependence
frameworks.summary: An ethical framework grounded in the shared material vulnerability and precarity of human bodies, demanding collective responsibility and nonviolence.
frameworks.details: Because humans share the air, surfaces, and infrastructures of the world, our persistence is inherently social. This framework argues that global obligations cannot rest on mere identification or sympathy, but on the unchosen reality that the world is given to us in common.
frameworks.representative_quote: This social character of our persistence and our possible flourishing means that we have to take collective responsibility for overcoming conditions of induced precarity.

mental_models.label: Gender Performativity
mental_models.summary: Viewing gender not as an innate fact or fake performance, but as a 'performative' act that brings reality into being, similar to a judge declaring a couple married.
mental_models.details: Unlike theatrical acting where the act is de-realized ('just a play'), gender performance in real life lacks these boundaries and carries real punitive risks for deviating from the script.
mental_models.representative_quote: when we talk about performative, we're talking about an act that makes something come into being or an act that has real consequences.

mental_models.label: The Psychosocial Phantasm
mental_models.summary: A psychological projection that serves a social and political function by collecting anxieties, enlarging fears, and misdirecting them toward a scapegoat.
mental_models.details: For example, the anti-gender movement treats 'gender' as an empty umbrella term to absorb massive economic and social anxieties, framing it as a demonic threat without needing the claims to logically cohere.
mental_models.representative_quote: gender is a fairly empty term what it does is it serves as a kind of umbrella concept under which all these social movements are are included

mental_models.label: Inadvertent Confession (Authoritarian Projection)
mental_models.summary: A mechanism where an attacking group accuses their target of the exact oppressive actions they themselves are committing.
mental_models.details: Authoritarian governments claim 'gender ideology' will strip people of their rights, while they actively strip trans people and women of their legal rights and bodily autonomy.
mental_models.representative_quote: Most of these allegations that are made by the Trump Administration are also confessions of what they themselves are doing in the very act.

mental_models.label: Furious Nostalgia / Fantasy of Restoration
mental_models.summary: Understanding right-wing populist appeal as a deeply emotional, aggressive desire to restore a lost social hierarchy and dominance, rather than mere economic anxiety.
mental_models.details: It explains why alienated workers are drawn to promises of returning to a time when 'men were men' and white men were unburdened by equality measures.
mental_models.representative_quote: The resentment thing is huge as is what I would call a furious Nostalgia just furious Nostalgia like I really want that back I really want my place back.

mental_models.label: Embodied Performativity
mental_models.summary: Viewing the physical assembly of bodies and public demonstrations as a form of political expression and demand-making, especially when speech is absent or marginalized.
mental_models.details: The body is not a static object but an active process of embodying cultural possibilities. Demonstrations document the failures of justice through the physical presence of bodies.

mental_models.label: Ecstasy (Being Beside Oneself)
mental_models.summary: A lens for viewing community and collectivity not through static identity, but through shared experiences of being overwhelmed or displaced by extreme emotion (grief, rage, passion) or shared crisis.
mental_models.details: It illustrates the fundamental dependency individuals have on others, binding people together into a community of strangers responding to a shared predicament.
mental_models.representative_quote: In a sense, a predicament is to understand what kind of community is composed of those who are beside themselves.

mental_models.label: Strategic Abandonment
mental_models.summary: The state's withdrawal from providing social welfare, forcing populations into precarity while shifting the burden of survival entirely onto the traditional family and churches.
mental_models.details: This creates the conditions where the traditional family is propped up as the only safety net, making any perceived threat to the family (like 'gender') feel like a threat to survival itself.
mental_models.representative_quote: as neoliberal economic policies devastate the work lives and the sense of Futurity for many people... the turn against gender is a way of shoring up a traditional sense of place and privilege

mental_models.label: The Materiality of Absence
mental_models.summary: Viewing absences not merely as empty spaces or missing information, but as active, material forces that shape and govern what is actually present.
mental_models.details: Used to demonstrate how missing elements, unmourned lives, or erased histories actively define the whole of society.
mental_models.representative_quote: absences are more than merely meaningful; rather, they are the material that governs that which is present.

heuristics.label: Doing One's Body
heuristics.summary: One is not simply a body, but, in some very key sense, one does one's body.
heuristics.representative_quote: One is not simply a body, but, in some very key sense, one does one's body.

heuristics.label: Reality Through Performance
heuristics.summary: Gender reality is real only to the extent that it is performed.
heuristics.representative_quote: Gender reality is performative which means, quite simply, that it is real only to the extent that it is performed.

heuristics.label: Making Trouble
heuristics.summary: Trouble is inevitable; the task is how best to make it and what best way to be in it.
heuristics.representative_quote: Trouble is inevitable and the task, how best to make it, what best way to be in it.

heuristics.label: Stumbling as Learning
heuristics.summary: Stumbling and making errors is a necessary part of learning new language and ways to respect others.
heuristics.representative_quote: But stumbling is part of learning and making an error is part of learning, especially when we're learning something new.

heuristics.label: Worldless Without Others
heuristics.summary: We are worldless without one another; decision only happens in the context of a prior entanglement.
heuristics.representative_quote: We are worldless without one another.

heuristics.label: Misnamed Fears
heuristics.summary: When people fear destruction, look at what is being misnamed as the cause of that fear.
heuristics.representative_quote: We have every reason to fear destruction. It is the cause of the fear of destruction that is being misnamed.

heuristics.label: Authoritarian Confessions
heuristics.summary: Most allegations made by authoritarian administrations are actually confessions of what they themselves are doing in the very act.
heuristics.representative_quote: Most of these allegations that are made by the Trump Administration are also confessions of what they themselves are doing in the very act.

heuristics.label: Violence as Self-Destruction
heuristics.summary: To do violence to another is also to violate something about your own humanity.
heuristics.representative_quote: To do violence to another is also to violate something about your own Humanity or to lose sight of it or to destroy it even.

heuristics.label: Sexy Freedom
heuristics.summary: Make freedom exciting and sexy to counter authoritarianism.
heuristics.representative_quote: Let us imagine freedom in a way we have not before. Make it exciting. Make it sexy.

signature_quotes.label: Gender as Stylized Repetition
signature_quotes.summary: Defining the core concept of gender performativity.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: gender is in no way a stable identity or locus of agency from which various acts proceede; rather, it is an identity tenuously constituted in time-an identity instituted through a stylized repetition of acts.

signature_quotes.label: The Anticipation of Essence
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining how the expectation of gender produces the phenomenon.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: the anticipation of a gendered essence produces that which it posits as outside itself.

signature_quotes.label: The Contradictory Phantasm
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining how the antigender movement weaponizes the concept of gender.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: The contradictory character of the phantasm allows it to contain whatever anxiety or fear that the anti-gender ideology wishes to stoke for its own purposes, without having to make any of it cohere.

signature_quotes.label: Assault on Democracy
signature_quotes.summary: Connecting the political attacks on gender to broader democratic threats.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: the assault on gender is also an assault on democracy.

signature_quotes.label: The Counter Social Movement
signature_quotes.summary: Defining the true political nature of the backlash against gender.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: the anti gender ideology movement is what I would call a counter social movement... its aim is to destroy a movement that it sees as gaining power gaining legislative victories

signature_quotes.label: Worldless Without One Another
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing how the world is never given to us alone, but always in the company of others.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: We are worldless without one another.

signature_quotes.label: The Confessional Operation
signature_quotes.summary: Describing the projection used by anti-gender activists.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: When I say that this operation is confessional, what I mean is that those who imagine that something called gender will take all your rights away are attributing to gender what they themselves are doing, namely taking rights away.

signature_quotes.label: Violence and Humanity
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing how boys and men are internally damaged by the brutal masculine norms they are compelled to enact.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: To do violence to another is also to violate something about your own Humanity or to lose sight of it or to destroy it even.

signature_quotes.label: Imagining Freedom
signature_quotes.summary: Advising the political left on how to counter right-wing rhetoric.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Let us imagine freedom in a way we have not before. Make it exciting. Make it sexy.

signature_quotes.label: Shared Vulnerability
signature_quotes.summary: Discussing the reciprocal and material modes of human vulnerability.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Humans share the air with one another and with animals; they share the surfaces of the world.

anti_patterns.label: The Expressive Model of Gender (Biological Essentialism)
anti_patterns.summary: Believing that gender acts, postures, and gestures are expressions of a pre-existing, internal, biological, or psychological core.
anti_patterns.details: It obscures the fact that gender is performatively constructed. Postulating a 'true' gender identity is a regulatory fiction used to control and punish those who deviate, forcing gender to comply with a false model of truth and falsity.

anti_patterns.label: Scapegoating 'Gender' for Neoliberal Failures
anti_patterns.summary: Blaming marginalized groups and 'gender ideology' for systemic economic failures and societal anxieties.
anti_patterns.details: It misdirects anger away from the actual culprits—corporate power, deregulated capitalism, and the decimation of social services—onto trans people, migrants, and DEI programs.

anti_patterns.label: Trans-Exclusionary Feminism
anti_patterns.summary: Relying on the category of 'woman' as an unexamined universal presupposition and excluding trans individuals from feminist solidarity.
anti_patterns.details: Under the guise of protecting women's rights, it insists gender is dictated entirely by biological sex, denying the Womanhood of trans individuals and reproducing the very binary gender identities that feminism seeks to dismantle.

anti_patterns.label: Restricting Sympathy to Likeness
anti_patterns.summary: Basing ethical obligations solely on feelings of identification or likeness to oneself.
anti_patterns.details: It restricts sympathy to those who are like us and thwarts our ability to respond ethically to alterity, those we fear, or those who are distant, ignoring that the world is given to us in common.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: If the self is the basis of sympathy, our sympathy will be restricted to those who are like us.

anti_patterns.label: The Nature/Culture Dualism
anti_patterns.summary: Treating nature and culture as a strict dichotomy or one-way causal model.
anti_patterns.details: It ignores that biology and environment are interactive; even biological markers are activated by environmental factors and social interactions, falsely positioning gender theory as 'antiscience.'

anti_patterns.label: Ahistorical Acceptance of the Gender Binary
anti_patterns.summary: Accepting the gender binary as a natural, pre-colonial default.
anti_patterns.details: It ignores historical evidence that the strict binary and heterosexual reproductive marriage were imposed by colonial powers and Christian missionaries, erasing diverse indigenous frameworks.

anti_patterns.label: Prescriptive Subversion
anti_patterns.summary: Prescribing a new 'subversive' way to live gender as a political mandate.
anti_patterns.details: It creates new exclusionary norms and hierarchies. Furthermore, subversive performances run the risk of becoming deadening cliches through repetition and commodification.

anti_patterns.label: Righteous Infighting over Joyful Vision
anti_patterns.summary: The political left becoming 'nerdy,' 'righteous,' and engaging in constant infighting instead of building a compelling vision.
anti_patterns.details: It distracts from the crucial work of building a passionate vision for the future and fails to unleash the positive energy needed to win people over against authoritarianism.
